pick.rationale: UConn enters as the favorite in this Sweet 16 matchup, leveraging their 2-seed status and Dan Hurley's recent championship DNA against Michigan State's 3-seed profile. The Huskies' superior tournament seeding reflects a stronger overall season performance, and their dominant Round of 32 victory over UCLA demonstrates they are peaking at the right time. While Tom Izzo's legendary tournament pedigree gives Michigan State a credible upset path and this matchup between elite coaches should be competitive, UConn's talent level, recent national championship experience, and the quality gap reflected in the seed differential give them the edge in this East Region contest.

reasoningStep.title: UConn vs Michigan State Sweet 16 Evaluation
reasoningStep.summary: UConn holds advantages in seeding, recent championship pedigree, and overall season performance, though Michigan State's tournament DNA under Tom Izzo presents legitimate upset danger in this competitive power conference matchup.
reasoningStep.evidence: UConn entered as a 2-seed with Dan Hurley seeking his third national championship in four seasons, while Michigan State was a 3-seed relying on Tom Izzo's tournament experience. Prior bracket picks showed UConn defeating UCLA in Round of 32 (73% confidence) and Michigan State edging Louisville (61% confidence), suggesting UConn has looked more dominant. Multiple bracket predictions favored UConn in this Sweet 16 matchup, and the Huskies' recent championship DNA provides a significant psychological and experiential advantage in high-pressure tournament situations.

You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.

pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
